untranslatable_reason: Adam & Eve Double Bottom is a complex multi-bar pattern recognition rule requiring: (1) detection of two distinct bottoms with specific spike characteristics across multiple bars, (2) comparison of spike heights/magnitudes between Adam and Eve bottoms, (3) assessment of price congestion width near each bottom. The allowed indicators do not include pattern-specific metrics like 'spike_height', 'congestion_width', or multi-bar bottom detection. The pattern requires visual/structural identification that cannot be reliably expressed with only price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, and volume indicators without additional pattern-detection logic.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using lower_wick (Adam spike), prev_low comparison (Eve shorter spike), and bb_bandwidth (congestion width proxy), but this oversimplifies the pattern. A proper implementation would require a dedicated pattern-detection indicator or custom multi-bar logic not available in the allowed indicator set. Exit condition uses simple moving average crossover as a reasonable reversal proxy per Rule 8.
